title: Hollow Knight
summary: Atmospheric exploration with tight combat and unforgettable boss fights.
body: Team Cherry delivered a world that feels hand-crafted in every screen. The difficulty curve is fair, upgrades feel meaningful, and the soundtrack carries emotional weight. Silksong cannot come soon enough.

title: Elden Ring
summary: Open-world Souls at its most ambitious — overwhelming in the best way.
body: The Lands Between reward curiosity. Some legacy dungeons feel copy-pasted, but peaks like Stormveil and Leyndell justify the journey. Co-op jank aside, this is a landmark action RPG.

title: Hades
summary: Roguelike structure with character writing that raises the bar.
body: Every run inches the story forward. Weapons feel distinct, boons create wild synergies, and the voice cast sells the family drama. Perfect for short sessions or long binge nights.

title: Tunic
summary: Isometric Zelda vibes with a manual you actually want to read.
body: The in-world instruction booklet is a genius narrative device. Combat is snappy, secrets are layered, and the late-game twists land hard if you have been paying attention.

title: Slay the Spire
summary: The deckbuilder roguelike that defined a genre.
body: Four characters, thousands of card combos, and the Spire never plays the same twice. Time sink warning: one more floor syndrome is real.
